transcript.text: Partnership could also just like go into the contract together and talk through like where we can meet in the middle, where there's not much flexibility.
I'm happy to take it either direction.
Yeah, cool.
So I will say, so I did take a brief look at the proposal you guys sent over.
I did not take the most in-depth look.
So if there's anything specific you want to call out, happy to chat through things.
Um, I think I have it open right now.
Or wait, let me see.
Am I?
Yeah, no.
Oh, here's the SoW.
Um, but yeah, I guess, um, I do have a couple questions just as we think about like what the first, yeah, like week would look like, first two weeks, blah, blah, blah.
Um, and then I'm trying to find it.
I can't.
Let's see.
Is it the...
the...
here.
Let's see.
You
Where can I find the one that had the, like, pricing and stuff?
Yeah, for sure.
Let me just pull something up for you.
Cool.
Okay.
So it's in that overview presentation.
It's like slide 18.
Okay, okay, okay.
I just haven't scrolled to it enough.
Okay.
Let me pull that up.
And then I've also got, like, a week-by-week of the eight weeks that we can talk about, and the pricing is also in the SoW.
Okay.
Wait, if I go to growth, is it, or no, I think I'm at the growthx overview.
Let's see.
I think it's, like, not showing in my emails.
Let me see.
Here you go.
We can just use this.
We can work off this one.
Cool.
Yeah, I mean, I guess, like, one of our initial reactions, to be honest.
It's just that this is, like, the proposal was, like, significantly above, honestly, what we expected and what we'd heard from other people to expect pricing-wise.
And we are quite cost-conscious at the moment.
We're, like, really focused on profitability.
And we just made a number of hires.
And so we, like, really are focused on making sure we're not, like, astronomically increasing our costs elsewhere.

So, honestly, I guess that that's, like, our most initial concern and reaction is just, like, the pricing was higher than we expected and higher than what we'd heard from, like, other people, like, other customers.
So I guess I'm curious if there's, yeah, like, yeah.
I'm curious what your initial reaction to that is.
Yeah, totally.
This is, like, our list pricing today for new customers that, like, have no connection to GrowthX.
We definitely are looking to charge this for the services that we're providing, which also includes, like, unlimited access to.
A platform that we'll set up and manage on your behalf.
But given that Felicia is engaged to the person at Tavern who's working directly with us, there's a lot of flexibility here.
So I guess the first thing I want to confirm is, do you know if you and Felicia are feeling confident enough to move forward if we can align on the right cost?
Are we at that place?
I don't want to like rush you all by any means, but we like fill up our kickoff calendar really quick.
And I know that I think she mentioned like in that last call, like how soon could we get started?
So if we can align on price, are we going to be in a place to sort of move forward with the partnership?
Yeah, I think I would need to take like the final proposal back to the team.
But, you know, we do want to fundraise soon.
With that, we have a number of different like things that we want to get started and government can move slow.
So I think we would want.
The things that I would say are going to be standard are with termination, we don't do termination for convenience for any client, but we do do termination for breach.
So we would need to keep the termination language the same.
And then we can definitely be flexible on other parts of the terms with the red lines that your team sends over.
And then the last piece here I would say with pricing is definitely can cut quite a bit off of this.
I guess I just sort of need to know like what the team could move forward with and then propose that to Marcel.
So I guess, you know, let me know what the proposal is and then I'll take it to him and let you know where we land.
Okay.
Yeah, I don't know if right now I have an exact number.

I think it's like I know that this is significantly above what we were expecting, but I think I can take it back to the team and also confirm with our like finance team, because I know they're doing a bunch of financial analyses right now.
Thank you.
In of what would be like a deal breaker versus okay on pricing.
Yeah, that's perfect.
Yeah.
Feel free to like ask Felicia directly also.
And if y'all could send us that proposed discount over by tomorrow, then I can get you confirmation before the end of the week on what we can agree to.

That way, the only thing left is really just to redline, go through the legal review with the rest of the terms in the contract, and then make sure that we could get to signature by the end of next week to go ahead and keep that March 2nd date.
But yeah, it should be it should be pretty easy once we hear from you on where you'd like to be and try to try our best to like either meet you there or meet you as close to it as we can.
Okay, cool.
Awesome.
So yeah, I'll do that.
And I'll make sure I send it back to you guys.
And then I guess the other question that I had, and I think Felicia might have more context, because I know she's talked to Pedro a bunch, and he's, you know, obviously worked with you guys.
But I guess I...
I'm curious to understand more of like the exact like way it would work.
So I guess as an example, like right now we're working with a PR agency in Kaus and it's like, you know, we meet once a week.
I have a Slack channel with them, but it's like they'll do outreach to media, bring it back to us, they'll draft award responses, things like that.

Like, guess just curious of like the very tactical of how this would work and like what would be, I think it's somewhere in here potentially, but like what would be the expectation in the first 30 days?
Again, I do think like we are planning to fundraise.
And so I, yeah, I guess I'm curious, like if we were to fundraise a month after this started or two months after it started, like what would be a reasonable aspiration to have?
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, in terms of the relationship, we'll set up a shared Slack channel.
We'll set up a shared notion space.
I can give you like a glimpse of how active we are, right?
With lovable, you can see we're.
Pretty much posting every day or, you know, close to it.
We're in a bit of an operating cadence where it's really like sending updates over, sending drafts of articles over, and sort of going through things that way.
But you can see like even Marcel, right, is jumping in and getting involved.
And when you look at the space that we've created with them, can give you a look at the Notion side of the house.
Um, I want to go to lovable.
You'll see that we're keeping all of these different docs that are helping us like understand their personas, helping us understand their products, their features.
We're doing, I don't want to email Sarah.
We're looking into like the competitive landscape.
There's a lot of just different docs that we're going to put together at the beginning of the partnership that your team will have access to or will even.
And use in the actual artifacts that we create in the workspace.
And so this is just like a look at a lot of docs that we've produced for them.
And then when you dig into the meetings, you know, every single meeting that we have, like we're taking a lot of notes.
And we're also doing performance reports where we set up like a Looker dashboard.
And we also give you summaries on how the content performance has been.
For over that time period.
So a lot of like close communication with you.
But the idea is that we really are not trying to take up more time of yours than we need to.
So what we'll do is we'll create version one of the artifacts in week one.
We'll calibrate on those with you.
We'll get feedback.
And then we'll start to create articles by week four.
And we'll definitely want your team to drop a lot of comments into the Google Docs, right?
So that we kind of know where.
We're hitting the mark where we need to fine-tune the engine, where we need to change context.

But that really is like the relationship in a nutshell, right, is helping us get the artifacts right and then helping us as we create our first articles whilst being able to see, you know, here are like the 70 articles that we've approved.
And as soon as GrowthX has decided which ones to prioritize, they'll send us over drafts, they'll let us know in advance which five articles those will be.
And then we give them feedback and once we've approved it, they'll go in our CMS and they'll stage and publish for us so that we don't have to worry about any of the additional steps in the process.
Okay, cool.

And, right, so the content that you guys are creating are all posted to our website as like blog posts or, yeah, I guess I'm curious to see an example of like for Lovable, let's say, like what's an example of a content piece that you guys put out?
Yeah, definitely.
So for Lovable, we had one work stream that was...
Very custom in nature, which was building out the templates library.
That was one where we created a totally unique pipeline that would interact with their API to create an app that folks could go in and preview or even remix.
And so we've done like the design on these pages, but that was, you know, separate from what we'd be doing in this engagement with you.
What we'd be doing in this engagement with you is similar to what we did in the guides section of their site.
And so a lot of our partners, not everyone, but some of them, since we're creating content at such a large scale, they sometimes want us to publish to the footer, right?
So instead of having it in the main menu nav, they're getting all of the search benefits because it can be indexed just the same way it would be indexed if it was in your main menu.
But it's not maybe cluttering like content that's more specific to your brand, your company, your products and features.
But is like really good answers to questions that people will search or keywords that people will, you know, use when they're searching.
So all of these articles across these different topic clusters, app alternatives, app inspiration, best apps and tools, we created all these for Lovable.
But you can also see like for Sentinel One, they wanted us to create this like cybersecurity 101 section.
And so they created the template for us, and then we created every individual post.
And so if you wanted us to create a new section of your site, we would just ask that you set that up for us and have the template that we could publish to.
But the template can be really simple.
It just needs to have a table of contents for better navigability, better user experience.
And it just needs to have the ability to insert, you know, an author, a date, and then tables if we wanted to bring tables in, which perform, you know, well with LLMs in particular.
Thank you.
So, yeah, Sentinel-1, Cybersecurity 101, Augment, not to the blog, but to the Guides, Learn, and Tools section of their site.
And then Airbyte, we are publishing directly to their blog, or no, is it, no, it's Data Engineering, that's where, where is the Data Engineering, here we go.
Data Engineering Resources, which is a different section of their site as well.
Really just more like personal preference, I would say.
Like some companies, like DataGrid, we published directly to their blog for everything.
There's like 944 articles, right?
So it just kind of depends on like how you want it to show up on your site.
Cool.

And then the goal is like, yeah, like if I was to then, you know, if you guys did a post on Binti, that then like if someone goes into Google and searches like social services, blah, blah, blah, like that Binti.
This article would pop up as one of the recommended ones.

Everything that we do is going to come from the keyword analysis so that we can actually see that there's search volume connected to it and that the competitor content is something that we feel like we can show up in front of.
Some of it, right, like it's not going to immediately show up in front of, but it's going to be worthwhile to produce.
And so there'll be a combination.
Some areas like where we'll immediately drive clicks impressions and impressions.

Others where we're going to try to be eating away at the authority of other content that may just come from much stronger domain authority, but might not be researched to the level that we can research it and written to the quality that we can write it.
Okay, cool.
And then...
And I will say this too.
This could also be like a great use case for y'all.
Like, discern, we had like this programmatic use case where for every single state, there's a page that we've created with information about like the franchise tax policy.
say too.
And And

So that could be like a really easy content play for us to execute on for you, where there's like rules based on the legislation in that state for like the services that you're helping to facilitate for these agencies.
Cool.

And then I guess like a tactical question of like, so our team right now, our website is built on WordPress, which we are looking into if we should like change it or whatever, because we feel like it can be a bit outdated.
But we do use WordPress right now.
And we have like contractors who update our website, who are abroad.
And so I guess like, would you guys be directly adding things to WordPress?
Would you be sending things to them for them to add to our website?
Like, how should I think about that?
That's a good question.
So if we looked at the Vinti website today, and then we went to the blog section.
Is there a blog section?
Or is it like news?
Or is
There is a news page for, like, PR visits we get.
Have y'all created any of this kind of content before?
In terms of, like, what you guys would create?
Yeah, like, search-optimized content?
Not really.
I guess, maybe it's, or no, this is more like product stuff.
Okay.
Yeah, I think the short answer is that we would probably have the contractor create the template in WordPress, but that should be a very low lift for them.
Like, that should not cost a lot or take long.

It should be as simple as we want a blog, we want the ability to have a table of contents, insert a table, insert an author and a date, and then also, we want to be able to have a cover image for that article so that if people, you know, share it or if they see it on social.
There's an image connected to it.
Okay, cool.
They will say, so like, yeah, because we are looking into if we should go with an alternative.
They're okay.
I will say like, I was working with them recently to try to get our newsroom created and it was a bit rough.
So I'm just giving you that context that that might be something for us to think about and plan on.
And then, have That's good to know, yeah.
And we did work with like CRV Ventures.

They had an agency and they, as we were like going through legal, they basically like reached out to the agency and said like, hey, can you make sure GrowthX has a section of the site they can post to that has X, Y, and Z.
Okay.
And then I guess the other, maybe this is in the contract.
Like, you, I know that when we worked with our PR firm, for example, we did like a three-month pilot period or trial period sprint and then we signed on for like a full annual.
Is that something you guys have ever done?
done?
We allow you to opt out until the last week of phase one.
So that would be that trial period.
It's about 50 days.
And we let you see the quality we can hit with our articles and the roadmap of everything that we would execute on with that search volume connected.
So you have at least a good sense of can GrowthX write these articles at the quality that we would expect to be able to drive more visibility.
I've also got like a ton of case studies where I can show you like charts where organic traffic has spiked a ton.
But that is the way that we set up our model because we are like measured on software standards since you'll be having access to our platform at some point in the next 12 months.
And also we have a bunch of forward deployed resources.
So like as an early stage business, we just got to make sure that like whoever we're working with, we're working with for at least right six plus months.
And then we use that first phase is like the window.
Where, like, you can opt out if you're really just not feeling confident in the impact we can make.
Okay.
And then I guess, like, one other question that I had for you is, have you guys ever, maybe not, but have you guys ever, like, created, like, video content and stuff?
Or is it always text-based?
We haven't.
We'd be open to talking more about that in the future.
But we haven't done it for a client before, to my knowledge.
So I think you'd benefit the most from us executing on the content that will help you show up in search, drive traffic to your website.

And if we're doing really good on that, and we feel like we've exhausted a lot of the content opportunities, then we can sort of think about, like, what makes sense as the next thing that we can work on together?
Is it repurposing our content for social posts for your executives, right?
Is it creating a video, which, in that case, we'd probably, like, recommend one of our partners, since we're not...
We're really a video production agency.
But I think one benefit is we're pretty flexible, right?
You've seen a lot of the different types of content that we've created.

So if that's super important to you and we've done a great job on the editorial side, then we can think about, okay, well, what makes sense to expand to or what makes sense to switch to, right, if we don't want to continue doing the content on your site.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I think that those are all the questions I had on my site.
Anything else you wanted to make sure that we covered?
Otherwise, I know that I'll get back to you.
Actually, wait, I do have a quick question because I'm sure that you guys have experience with this.
Like, we are actually right now evaluating, like, different vendors of, like, do we build our own website on Lovable and have us do it or use Webflow or, like, keep our thing?
Do you have – I assume you guys have worked across a number of different website platforms.
Like, do you guys have any, I don't know, intel on what you found to be helpful or good?
Yeah, basically.
So…
What CMS platforms do we recommend?
yeah.
I will double check with the team, but I know that Webflow is one.
Framer is another one, especially earlier on.
That can be a good solution.
I'll see what else they recommend.
Sanity is good if you have developers involved that want to build out the infrastructure to make it super scalable.
But it might be more than you need.
I think Webflow and Framer would probably be the best ones to look into right now, and I'll let you know if they recommend any others.
Okay, that was just a random thought that I had as we were talking.
Awesome, okay, yeah, I think that those are the questions I had my side.
Anything else that we should cover or chat about?
No, I think we're good.
Just let me know if there's any other questions that come up that I can answer over email or any additional resources that you need as you talk internally.
But I think if we can get the first proposal from Felicia and team on what you'd want to see.
The list price discounted too.
We can give you a quick answer on where we can land by end of the day tomorrow.

And then we can, assuming we're aligned there, we can make sure that we get the legal review finished, hopefully by like Wednesday or Thursday next week so that we can sign for the second right after.
So those will be like the next steps that we take.
And if y'all need us to answer any questions or jump on another call, just let us know sooner rather than later.
Because we're like actively, you know, my mandate for Marcel is I want us to be four to eight weeks out so that we have a wait list.
And like we're in the process of doing that right now.
And I think like we want to work with Binti, obviously, like it's a use case that's like very near and dear to Marcel.
And like personally, I think it's great what y'all are doing.
So I think if we can just like align on the price and finalize the contract by end of next week, we're going to be in a great position.
And that is for both the growth execution and access to the platform together or just the growth execution?
Exactly.
The total price.
Okay.

And then I guess like the other thing is on the one-time fee that I'm just thinking of is I know that our CFO has been looking right now and like trying to keep our profitability steady and we just did a big hiring sprint.
So we've like done hiring bonuses recently and like there's been a big like set of one-time payouts.
So I'm assuming from our head of finance, that actually might be the piece that makes her more nervous.
Oh, yeah.
So I guess.
Yeah.
Yeah.
On that piece.
Yeah.
I mean, I think it's going to be a combination, right, of like one or the other or a little bit of both.
But I definitely think we can do 30 for the phase one with a level of complexity, right?
We're like working directly with, you know, you and Felicia essentially.
And you're starting from zero here.
So there's like a.
There's lot of room for us to dictate the right strategy and not have to calibrate so much on what strategy makes the most sense to execute on.
So I think there's wiggle room on both sides and hopefully some of the numbers I gave you give you a starting point.
Okay, cool.
All right.
Thank you.
Yeah, no problem.
Look forward to hearing back from you tomorrow and we'll keep this thing moving.
Here to help them.
Thank you.
Bye.
Thanks, Rima.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[To align on pricing and operational details for the GrowthX partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Pricing is the main blocker.** Binti finds the proposal "significantly above" expectations and is cost-conscious after a recent hiring sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)
  - [**Significant discount is possible.** GrowthX will offer a discount on both the monthly fee and one-time setup fee, citing a personal connection (Felicia is engaged to a GrowthX contact) as the reason for flexibility.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=223.0)
  - [**Binti to propose a target price by Feb 13.** GrowthX will provide a final answer by Feb 14, aiming for a contract signature by Feb 20 to meet the March 2 kickoff.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)
  - [**The model is a 50-day trial within a 6+ month commitment.** Binti can opt out before the end of Phase 1 (approx. 50 days) if not satisfied with the content quality and roadmap.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1121.0)

## Topics

### Pricing & Contract Negotiation

  - [Binti's primary concern is the proposal's price, which is "significantly above" expectations.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Binti is highly cost-conscious and focused on profitability after a large hiring sprint.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=177.0)
  - [GrowthX confirmed flexibility on pricing due to the personal connection.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=223.0)
      - [**Offer:** A significant discount on both the monthly fee and the one-time setup fee.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1511.0)
      - [**Constraint:** The contract's "termination for breach" clause is non-negotiable, as it is standard for all clients.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Binti's CFO is particularly concerned about the one-time setup fee, as the company just incurred other large one-time payouts (e.g., hiring bonuses).](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1511.0)

### Partnership Model & Workflow

  - [**Communication:** A shared Slack channel and Notion workspace for daily updates, drafts, and feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=528.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
    1.  [**Weeks 1–4:** Develop foundational artifacts (personas, competitive analysis) and calibrate with Binti.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
    2.  [**Week 4+:** Begin drafting articles. Binti provides feedback via Google Docs comments.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=642.0)
    3.  [**Publishing:** GrowthX stages approved articles in Binti's CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=677.0)
  - [**Content Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=889.0)
      - [**Goal:** Drive organic traffic via search-optimized content.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=889.0)
      - [**Method:** Keyword analysis targets high-volume terms and opportunities to outrank competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=889.0)
      - [**Format:** Text-based articles (e.g., guides, programmatic content). Video is not included but can be discussed later.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1207.0)

### Technical Implementation (WordPress)

  - [Binti's site is on WordPress; a potential platform migration is being evaluated.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=975.0)
  - [**Requirement:** Binti's contractors must create a simple WordPress template for GrowthX to publish into.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1044.0)
      - [**Template needs:** Table of Contents, author/date fields, table support, and a cover image.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1044.0)
  - [**Context:** Binti noted past difficulties with its contractors, which may require careful planning for this step.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1083.0)
  - [**CMS Recommendations:** GrowthX suggested Webflow and Framer as good alternatives to WordPress for Binti's needs.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=1292.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Binti:** Propose a target price for the monthly fee and one-time setup fee by **Feb 13**.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Provide a final answer on the proposed price by **Feb 14**.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)
  - [**Both:** If pricing aligns, complete legal review by **Feb 19** and sign the contract by **Feb 20** to meet the **March 2** kickoff date.](https://fathom.video/share/xqhZa-_LXW_zTKCgLNSXDBsJYfJRCjA5?tab=summary&timestamp=430.0)

action_items.description: Email Tyler proposed total discount (incl. one-time) by Feb 13; then Tyler confirm by EOD
Email Rima CMS recommendations (Webflow, Framer, Sanity) by Feb 13
